name: Share Transfer Restrictions — RoFR and RoFO
text: No Shareholder shall transfer, assign, pledge, or otherwise dispose of any shares held by it without the prior written consent of the other Shareholders, except as permitted under this Agreement. Before transferring any shares to a third party, the transferring Shareholder ('Transferor') shall first offer those shares to the remaining Shareholders on a pro-rata basis ('Right of First Refusal') at the same price and on the same terms as proposed for the third-party transfer. The remaining Shareholders shall have thirty (30) days from receipt of the transfer notice to exercise their Right of First Refusal. Any transfer made in breach of this clause shall be void and of no legal effect.

legal_basis.note: Right to transfer shares — subject to Articles
Transfer of shares — instrument of transfer required
Fair market value for unlisted share transfers

invalid_if: Transfer restrictions are not clearly defined
Right of first refusal period is not specified
title: Share Transfer Restrictions and Right of First Refusal

jurisdiction_constraints.general: Section 50CA: transfers below FMV trigger deemed FMV valuation for tax purposes

dispute_hotspots: RoFR not in Articles — third party buyer not bound by SHA restriction
S.50CA: below-FMV transfer taxed as if at FMV
Stamp duty on share transfer — stamp duty on transfer form
